section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports feeling bone spurs and a grinding sensation in the joint upon palpation and range of motion testing.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This sentence is framed as patient-reported sensation rather than examiner-observed findings; however, it is closely tied to the exam maneuver and does not substantially mislead.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports that the sheets bother the toe when he is in bed at night.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a subjective symptom description and belongs in the history rather than the objective findings section, but its duplication here is unlikely to confuse clinical interpretation.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports that the pain is exacerbated by pushing in on the joint and moving it back and forth.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This describes symptom provocation as experienced by the patient, fitting HPI; in Findings it is slightly misleading but still understandable in context of exam maneuvers.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports feeling pain when the toe is flexed to 20 degrees of plantar flexion.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a subjective pain response rather than an objective measurement; while linked to an exam position, it is more appropriately part of the symptom history.
